title: Liver Consultants — Initial Visit

postNotes:
Weight 139 lbs, BP 105/64
First meeting with Mahgoub. Reviewed overall liver and cancer status.

title: Oncology — Immunotherapy #1
postNotes:
Weight 140.8 lbs, BP 124/70, Temp 98.5, HR 67
First immunotherapy session. 1 dose of 2 drugs via IV. No pre-medication. Every 4 weeks.

title: Oncology — Immunotherapy #2

postNotes:
Weight 136.8 lbs, BP 134/70
Labs + Abushahin appt + immunotherapy completed. New narco prescription.

title: Psychiatry — Follow Up
postNotes: Prescribed: Cymbalta 20mg (×2), Remeron 15mg at bedtime. Follow up in 1 month.

title: Oncology — Immunotherapy #3
postNotes: Spoke with dietician. Increase caloric intake recommended.

title: Psychiatry — Follow Up
postNotes: Dad sleeping 3–4 hrs/night max. Remeron increased to 30mg. Call if no improvement in 10 days. May add Seroquel next. Cymbalta same dosage. Next appt 02/05 11AM.

title: Oncology — Immunotherapy #4
postNotes:
Weight 141 lbs, BP 110/60
Dad mentioned pain in a specific spot on belly.

title: Psychiatry — Follow Up
postNotes: Everything went well. Dad gave update on immunotherapy. Continue Seroquel and Remeron same dosage. Next appt April 30 at 11am.

title: Hepatology — Liver Follow Up
prep:
Questions:
- Status of liver?
- Plan to address liver issues?
- Continue acid medication?
- Share abdomen pain on right side (1+ month)
postNotes:
Temp 97.9, BP 119/69, HR 72

1. Cymbalta is hard on liver — Mahgoub asked psychiatrist to wean off or switch to gabapentin/lyrica.
2. Starting Entecavir (Baraclude) to prevent Hep B flare-up — must continue indefinitely.
3. Abdomen pain on right side: likely procedure, constipation, or chemo side effect. Use heating pad or Icy Hot.
4. Liver transplant earliest discussion: Sept/Oct 2025 after 1 year of treatment with no cancer evidence.

Next appt: May 19 at 11:30am.

title: Nephrology — Kidney Follow Up
postNotes: Blood work and urine test results good. Kidney functioning well, no complications. Doc concerned about weight loss (138.6 lbs). Next appt in 6 months.

title: Oncology — Immunotherapy #5
postNotes:
Weight 137.5 lbs (4 lbs less than last appt).
Clarified Mahgoub vs. Gupta confusion — Dad continuing with Abushahin for immunotherapy through rest of year, possibly longer.
CT scan to be scheduled before or day of next immunotherapy.
Next appt: 03/31 8:20am.

title: Oncology — Immunotherapy #6
postNotes:
Blood work normal. CT scan normal except back of abdomen (near pancreas) showing inflammation — lighter spot of unknown cause (cancer cells vs. chemo side effect).
Abushahin recommending advanced endoscopy biopsy. Will call Mahgoub to arrange.
Next immunotherapy #7: April 28, labs 8:30AM, appt 9AM.

title: Oncology — Immunotherapy #7
postNotes: Labs 8:30AM, Abushahin 9AM.

title: Psychiatry — Follow Up
postNotes: Follow up from Feb 5 appointment.

title: Hepatology — Liver Follow Up
postNotes: Weight 134.3 lbs. Overall numbers look good. WBC high 5% from 4/28 scan. Tissue looking different. During open enrollment, check that transplant coverage is added.

title: Oncology — Immunotherapy + May Scan Recap
postNotes:
May scan results:
- Original liver area (where it ruptured): smaller vs March — immunotherapy likely working.
- Back of abdomen tissue: worse vs March and vs October. More bulky/prominent. Unknown — could be cancer or treatment side effect. Tissue is between organs, hard to reach (vein blocking).

Next steps:
- Gupta to be consulted on how to sample the tissue
- Additional blood work ordered (tumor markers)

Symptoms to watch: loose stool, diarrhea, swelling legs/abdomen, intense back pain.
Next immunotherapy: 06/25 8AM labs, 9AM Laith.

title: Surgery Consult — Laparoscopy Planning
prep:
Questions:
- How long is the procedure?
- What are the risks?
- What is the goal?
- Who performs it?
- When is it scheduled?
- Post-procedure recovery?
- Next steps after — Gupta or Abushahin?
postNotes: Gupta consulted with Abushahin on May scan. Excisional lymph node biopsy planned to sample tissue in back of abdomen. Gupta nurse Megan called same day to schedule. Procedure: less than 1 hour.

title: Surgery — Excisional Lymph Node Biopsy
postNotes: Excisional lymph node biopsy completed. 8AM check-in.

title: Post-Op Follow Up
prep:
Questions:
1. Can dad mow grass without harming incisions?
2. Why did lymph node size increase — will it continue?
postNotes:
Weight 131.2 lbs, Temp 98.8

Gupta confirmed: sample from lymph node near pancreas is cancerous — same liver cancer cells. Not a different cancer type.
Gupta did not remove full lymph node — too invasive; goal was biopsy only.
Cannot rule out spread to other areas (only visible on scan once large enough).
Gupta informed Abushahin day of surgery.

Healing instructions: eat/drink, keep clean, don't pick the glue off.

Abushahin thinks Cabometyx may not be working given pain. Requesting Tibsovo (insurance may push back). Continue Cabometyx until Tibsovo ready.
Tibsovo targets IDH-1 mutation in liver/bile duct cancer.

Next appt: 9/17 8AM labs + Abushahin. Dietician to visit.
CT scan scheduled for week of Sept 20.

Met with dietician Morgan.
Oxycodone prescribed (every 6 hrs as needed) + Senakot morning & night for constipation.

Tibsovo: can be taken with food.

title: Labs — Blood Work
postNotes: Weight 121 lbs. Blood work went well. Scan planned for 3 weeks.

title: CT Scan + Hepatology Follow Up
prep:
Questions for Mahgoub:
1. Purpose of Omeprazole? How long to continue?
2. How long to take Entecavir?
3. Current status of liver?
postNotes:
Blood work + CT scan (7:30AM). Mahgoub appt 11:30AM.
Weight 124.7 lbs, BP 135/70

5 Key Takeaways:
1. Liver stable — ALT/AST normal, bilirubin normal, alkaline phosphatase good.
2. Entecavir critical — continue indefinitely to prevent Hep B flare-up.
3. Omeprazole optional — taper (every other day → stop). Can restart if needed.
4. Watch for: abdominal fluid, yellowing of skin/eyes, constant itching, confusion/brain fog.
5. Liver transplant: premature — need sustained cancer clearance first.

Next Mahgoub appt: March 31, 2026.

Entecavir + Omeprazole refill confirmed — FedEx delivery.

doctor: Dr. Bradley Dweck / Dr. Laith Abushahin
prep:
Questions:

postNotes:
Weight 118 lbs, BP 108/67, Temp 98
ANC 2.2 (better), WBC 3.6 (better), Potassium 5.1 (normal)
Dr. Bradley Dweck (fellow) + Abushahin

ANC normal enough — chemo/immuno infusion done today.
Potassium normal — likely stop potassium chloride (awaiting Abushahin confirmation).
Dad had nausea this morning — Zofran/Ondansetron as needed? Awaiting confirmation.

CT Scan (Mar 13): everything worse vs Sept 2025 (radiology compared to Sept scan, not Dec — needs correction).
Abushahin considering treatment schedule change. Will print all 3 scan comparisons.
Two populations of cancer cells: liver + bile duct.
